Ephraim satisfies himself with empty hopes of help from people, when he is at odds with God. The Jews vainly thought to secure the Egyptians by giving them products of their land. Judah is displeased as well. God sees the sin of his own people and will hold them accountable for it. They are reminded of what Jacob did and what God did for him. When his faith in the Divine promise overcame his fears, then by his strength he had power with God. He is Jehovah, the same who was, is, and is to come. What was a revelation of God to one person is his memorial to many, to all generations. Then let those who have turned away from God be turned back to him. Turn to the Lord through repentance and faith, as your God. Let those who are converted to him walk with him in all holiness and godliness. Let us struggle with Him for promised blessings, determined not to give up until we succeed; and let us seek Him in his appointments.

Ephraim became a merchant: the word also means a Canaanite. They carried on trade according to Canaanitish principles, greedily and with fraud and deceit. In this way they became rich and falsely supposed that God’s favor was upon them. But shameful sins will have shameful punishments. Let them remember not only what a mighty prince Jacob was with God, but also what a servant he was to Laban. The benefits we have had from the word of God make our sin and folly worse if we treat that word lightly. We are better off doing hard labor in poverty than growing rich through sin. We can judge our own conduct by comparing it with that of ancient believers in similar circumstances. Whoever rejects the message of God will perish. May we all hear his word with humble, obedient faith.
